evidence_snippets: Include TOC (!!)
coverage_entries.reason: Meeting notes explicitly require a TOC.

evidence_snippets: change log / revision history
coverage_entries.reason: Notes call for a change log / revision history section.

evidence_snippets: Intro section: must explain purpose
coverage_entries.reason: Intro must define purpose, conventions, and guide readers.

evidence_snippets: keep this doc as PRODUCT SPEC only
coverage_entries.reason: Multiple artifacts state the document is a product spec, not design.

evidence_snippets: Section layout: 2/4 desc; 3/5 interfaces/non-func
coverage_entries.reason: Section roles 2,3,4,5 are described in meeting notes.

evidence_snippets: Explicit note: read intro first
coverage_entries.reason: Notes explicitly instruct readers to read the intro first.

evidence_snippets: PMs will make decisions off Sections 3 (interfaces) + 5
coverage_entries.reason: PMs are directed to consult sections 3 and 5 for decisions.

evidence_snippets: Visual color chooser ('graphical picker')
coverage_entries.reason: Spec and transcript list a visual/graphical color chooser feature.

evidence_snippets: Translator: map old paint numbers + collections
coverage_entries.reason: Translator mapping old to new numbers and collections is specified.

evidence_snippets: Closest-colors finder; algorithm = RGB distance
coverage_entries.reason: Notes state a closest-colors finder using RGB distance assumption.

evidence_snippets: Color search engine
coverage_entries.reason: Spec repeatedly names a color search engine capability.

evidence_snippets: sample matcher
coverage_entries.reason: Sources describe an image/sample matcher feature.

evidence_snippets: User color palette: session-based
coverage_entries.reason: Meeting notes specify a session-based user palette feature.

evidence_snippets: Admin console
coverage_entries.reason: Admin console and user/paint management appear in the spec.

evidence_snippets: User interface (high-level only,
coverage_entries.reason: External interfaces (user/hardware/software/communications) are listed.

evidence_snippets: UI patterns are going into that separate whitepaper
coverage_entries.reason: UI details are explicitly placed in a separate whitepaper.

evidence_snippets: help pages on the site
coverage_entries.reason: End-user help and distributor docs are requested in sources.

evidence_snippets: deployment guide
coverage_entries.reason: Docs and deployment guide for Ops/IT management are referenced.

evidence_snippets: Assumptions & Dependencies
coverage_entries.reason: An 'Assumptions & Dependencies' section is mandated in notes.

evidence_snippets: translator needs to handle add/remove gracefully
coverage_entries.reason: Translator behavior for adds/discontinuations is explicitly required.

evidence_snippets: Provide CSS hooks for Jade
coverage_entries.reason: Spec requires theme hooks and CSS skinning.

evidence_snippets: each main feature on its own page
coverage_entries.reason: Module-per-page approach required.

evidence_snippets: map old paint numbers + collections to new
coverage_entries.reason: Translator handles mapping for migration.

evidence_snippets: we don't confuse customers during changeover
coverage_entries.reason: Marketing demands customer experience continuity.

evidence_snippets: Deliver as standalone server-based web application
coverage_entries.reason: App delivered as server-hosted web client.

evidence_snippets: All search processing + storage happen on server
coverage_entries.reason: Ops mandates server-side processing and storage.

evidence_snippets: 1 GHz CPU + 512 MB RAM per 50 users
coverage_entries.reason: Capacity guideline specified in constraints.

evidence_snippets: IE 4.01, Netscape 6.0, Mozilla 1.0+
coverage_entries.reason: Browser support list explicitly given.

evidence_snippets: Client machines meet browser vendor requirements
coverage_entries.reason: Clients must satisfy browser system requirements.

evidence_snippets: compatible with most operating systems
coverage_entries.reason: Spec states cross-OS compatibility for most OSes.

evidence_snippets: keyboard-only operation must be fully usable
coverage_entries.reason: Accessibility requirement explicitly states keyboard-only usability.

evidence_snippets: auto-send crash logs to us, ColorKast
coverage_entries.reason: Sources require both user report utility and automatic logs to ColorKast.

evidence_snippets: system shall attempt to fully recover from errors
coverage_entries.reason: Meeting notes and emails mandate attempting full recovery on errors.

evidence_snippets: Error reporting UX: non-intrusive. No blocking popups
coverage_entries.reason: Specified non-intrusive reporting and ban on blocking popups.

evidence_snippets: Default users (non-admin) can use all functionality except admin
coverage_entries.reason: Meeting notes define default/non-admin users with all non-admin access.

evidence_snippets: session-only, no password-protected storage, expires with session end
coverage_entries.reason: Notes state default user data is session-only and not password-protected.

evidence_snippets: Admin roles L1/L2/L3 with permissions
coverage_entries.reason: Documents enumerate admin levels L1–L3, implying hierarchical roles.

evidence_snippets: L1 = add + create L1; L2 = add/update + create up to L2; L3 = add/update/delete + create any level
coverage_entries.reason: Meeting notes explicitly list the permissions for L1, L2, and L3.

evidence_snippets: Document design & implementation constraints in SRS
coverage_entries.reason: Notes require documenting design and implementation constraints in SRS.

evidence_snippets: go live by end of Q2 2004
coverage_entries.reason: Email explicitly states business constraint to go live by Q2 2004.

evidence_snippets: assumed to be client-perceived metrics
coverage_entries.reason: Spec says untagged performance defaults to client.

evidence_snippets: will meet or exceed the requirements we specify
coverage_entries.reason: Sources state clients/servers must meet or exceed requirements.

evidence_snippets: intended primarily for rollout
coverage_entries.reason: Email notes this 'wide-installation' use as an assumption.

evidence_snippets: algorithm assumption = RGB distance acceptable
coverage_entries.reason: Meeting and vendor state RGB distance is acceptable for v1.

evidence_snippets: use 3rd party DB for paint data + search index
coverage_entries.reason: Notes and vendor emails state dependency on external paint DBs.

evidence_snippets: 'highly accessible to clients and distributors via the web'
coverage_entries.reason: Non-functional section explicitly calls for high accessibility.

evidence_snippets: compatible with most operating systems
coverage_entries.reason: Doc requires compatibility with specified hardware/OS/browser list.

evidence_snippets: version 1.0, no prior live revs at ABC
coverage_entries.reason: Emails explicitly state this is v1.0 with no prior live revisions.

unsupported_implied.text: The system shall not require client-side plugins (e.g., Flash) and must operate in regular browsers without plugin dependencies.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: Raj: Yes, the app is server-based. Clients are regular browsers — no plugins. ... Linda: We cannot alienate people on IE 4.x and Netscape 6. It's in our site compatibility promise. ... Maria: Please don't sneak any Flash in there.
unsupported_implied.reason: The participants explicitly require the application to run in standard browsers without relying on plugins (and explicitly call out avoiding Flash). The gold requirements do not state a prohibition on client-side plugins or an explicit "no-plugins" constraint; that is a significant implementation constraint affecting allowable client-side technologies.

unsupported_implied.text: The system shall provide print-friendly output or an in-app printing capability (e.g., printer-friendly stylesheet or print function) for user palettes and translator results so store staff can print palettes at the counter.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: Ben: So I can, as a store clerk, open the site, pick some colors for a customer, add them to a palette, print it if needed, but after I close the browser, it's gone. Fine.
unsupported_implied.reason: Stakeholders expect the application to let store staff print palettes/results directly from the tool. While the gold set requires distributor documentation and printable guides, it does not require the application itself to provide printer-friendly output or printing functionality for palettes/translator results; this is a distinct, significant user-facing capability implied by the source.
